skill.name: senior-backend
skill.description: Backend development skill for designing APIs, reviewing backend architecture, optimizing databases, and applying security practices across Node.js, Python, Go, REST, GraphQL, and PostgreSQL systems.
skill.summary: Design, review, and improve scalable backend systems.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: AI review dismissed the high-risk static matches as false positives caused by Markdown code fences, ordinary words such as description and design, and placeholder documentation. The only confirmed risk is low-severity local filesystem write behavior in optional report output, with no evidence of network access, credential exfiltration, prompt injection, or malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Optional Local Report File Write
low_findings.description: The Python scripts accept an output path and write JSON results when the user passes the JSON output option. This is expected CLI behavior, but it can overwrite a user-selected path if used carelessly.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file write is explicit and present in all three scripts. It is low risk because the path is supplied by the local user and no hidden write target or exfiltration path was found.

low_findings.title: Environment File Copy Documented
low_findings.description: The setup documentation suggests copying an example environment file to a local environment file. This is normal project setup guidance and does not read, print, or transmit secrets.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed line is a documentation command, not code that accesses environment secrets. No network or credential collection behavior was found nearby.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fences Flagged as Shell Backticks
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analysis flagged Markdown fenced code blocks and inline code spans as Ruby shell backtick execution. These locations are documentation examples, command snippets, or file path references and do not execute inside the skill files.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Every reviewed location is Markdown syntax or documentation text. No Ruby code, shell interpolation, or executable backtick expression is present.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Keywords
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptography because of substrings in ordinary words, headings, descriptions, and argument help text. No MD5, DES, SHA1, or cryptographic implementation was found at these locations.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The flagged locations contain plain documentation words or argparse description text. The scripts do not import or call cryptographic libraries.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analysis flagged anti-pattern documentation and reference headings as reconnaissance. The reviewed text is generic guidance and does not run host discovery commands.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The matched lines are Markdown headings and explanatory text. No commands such as uname, whoami, ipconfig, or process listing were found in those locations.

content.value_statement: Backend teams need consistent guidance for API design, database changes, security review, and performance work. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code structured prompts, references, and local helper scripts for backend tasks.
actual_capabilities: Guides API design reviews for REST, GraphQL, authentication, authorization, and backend business logic.
Provides reference material for database optimization, backend security practices, and API design patterns.
Includes local Python scripts that validate a target path and generate basic JSON analysis reports.
Supports backend planning across Node.js, Express, Python, Go, PostgreSQL, Prisma, Docker, and cloud platforms.
Helps structure prompts for scaffolding APIs, reviewing migrations, and identifying performance bottlenecks.
limitations: The bundled scripts are simple templates and do not perform real load testing or migration analysis yet.
Reference documents contain generic placeholder examples rather than complete framework-specific implementations.
The skill does not connect to live databases, cloud accounts, or production monitoring systems by itself.
Generated recommendations still need project-specific review, tests, and security validation before deployment.

use_cases.title: Plan a New Backend API
use_cases.description: Use the skill to define endpoints, data models, validation rules, authentication boundaries, and implementation steps before coding.

use_cases.title: Review Database Changes
use_cases.description: Use the database guidance to check migration plans, query patterns, indexing strategy, and rollback risks before release.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Security Review
use_cases.description: Use the backend security checklist to inspect input validation, authorization, secret handling, and logging behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Review an Endpoint Design
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this backend endpoint plan. Identify missing request validation, authentication checks, response shapes, error cases, and tests.

prompt_templates.title: Improve a Database Query
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this database query and schema context. Suggest indexing, pagination, transaction, and caching improvements with tradeoffs.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Migration Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a safe migration plan for this schema change. Include rollout steps, rollback strategy, data backfill risks, and verification checks.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Backend Service
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform a senior backend audit of this service. Cover API contracts, data access, authorization, observability, performance, and operational risks.

output_examples.input: Review a planned orders API for an ecommerce service.
output_examples.output: A structured review covering route design, validation, authorization rules, idempotency, error responses, and test cases.

output_examples.input: Assess a migration that adds billing fields to a large customer table.
output_examples.output: A rollout plan with backward-compatible steps, batching guidance, rollback checks, and monitoring recommendations.

output_examples.input: Find backend security gaps before a release.
output_examples.output: A prioritized checklist covering input validation, access control, secret handling, dependency review, logging, and abuse protection.

best_practices: Provide service context, data models, traffic expectations, and deployment constraints before asking for recommendations.
Ask for explicit tradeoffs when comparing API, database, caching, and security options.
Validate generated plans with tests, code review, and environment-specific security controls.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for production database changes without rollback requirements and data volume context.
Do not treat generic reference guidance as a replacement for framework documentation or threat modeling.
Do not run helper scripts against sensitive paths unless you understand the generated output destination.

faq.question: Does this skill generate complete backend applications?
faq.answer: It can help plan and scaffold backend work, but the bundled scripts are templates and need project-specific implementation.

faq.question: Can it review both REST and GraphQL APIs?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill description and prompts cover REST APIs, GraphQL APIs, validation, authentication, and authorization concerns.

faq.question: Does it connect to my database?
faq.answer: No. The skill provides guidance and local scripts, but it does not connect to live databases by default.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: The audit found low risk only. The confirmed behavior is optional local file output from user-run scripts.

faq.question: Why did static analysis report high risk?
faq.answer: Most high-risk matches were false positives from Markdown code fences and ordinary words that resembled security keywords.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
